model_narrative: We plan motor production across the seasons of a year. In each season we decide how many motors to produce, which shifts to operate and how many employees to put on each shift, how many employees to hire or fire, how much inventory to carry forward, and whether to take an extra-space lease for the year. Production in each season is set by interpolating along a piecewise-linear production curve. Costs accrue each season for materials and shift operation, for carrying inventory and leasing space, and for hiring and firing, and every season's cost is expressed in present value using a discount factor. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon.

sets.name: q
sets.doc: the seasons of the planning horizon in chronological order; the first member is the opening season and each later member immediately follows the one before it

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the work shifts that can be operated within a season

sets.name: l
sets.doc: the ordered production-rate levels of a piecewise-linear production curve, from lowest to highest; consecutive levels are adjacent breakpoints

sets.name: i
sets.doc: an auxiliary index set not referenced by any constraint in this model

params.name: d
params.doc: the number of motors demanded in each season, in motors; demand is zero until the final season when it must all be met

params.name: lc
params.doc: the leasing cost charged in each season if the extra-space lease is taken, in dollars per season

params.name: ei
params.doc: the externally given change to the workforce headcount entering each season, in employees; nonzero only in the opening season where it sets the starting workforce

params.name: delt
params.doc: the discount factor applied to each season's costs to express them in present value; equal to one in the opening season and decreasing in later seasons

params.name: mc
params.doc: the material cost incurred per motor produced, in dollars per motor

params.name: sr
params.doc: the space-rental cost charged per motor of inventory held beyond the leased allowance, in dollars per motor

params.name: hc
params.doc: the hiring cost incurred per employee hired, in dollars per employee

params.name: fc
params.doc: the firing cost incurred per employee fired, in dollars per employee

params.name: invmax
params.doc: the inventory allowance covered by the lease, in motors; equal to the total demand summed over all seasons

params.name: pr_motor
params.doc: the breakpoint coordinates of the piecewise-linear production curve at each level: the 'labor' row gives the employee-hours value and the 'motor' row gives the motors-produced value at that level; indexed first by the row label then by level

params.name: sc
params.doc: shift cost coefficients: the 'fixed' row gives the fixed cost of operating a shift and the 'labor' row gives the per-employee labor cost of a shift; indexed first by the row label then by shift

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total discounted cost over the whole horizon, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: dpc
vars.doc: the direct production cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: isc
vars.doc: the inventory-related cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: wfc
vars.doc: the workforce-adjustment cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: src
vars.doc: the space-rental charge in each season, in dollars

vars.name: p
vars.doc: the number of motors produced in each season

vars.name: ss
vars.doc: the interpolation weight placed on each production-curve level for a given season and shift; the weights select a point along the piecewise-linear curve

vars.name: ssb
vars.doc: an auxiliary binary indicator for the piecewise-linear segment of each season, shift, and level; not referenced by any constraint in this model

vars.name: inv
vars.doc: the inventory of motors carried at the end of each season

vars.name: lease
vars.doc: the lease decision; equals 1 if the extra-space lease is taken and 0 otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: the total workforce headcount employed in each season

vars.name: se
vars.doc: the workforce headcount assigned to each shift in each season

vars.name: shift
vars.doc: the shift-operation indicator; equals 1 if the shift is operated in that season and 0 otherwise

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the number of employees hired in each season

vars.name: f
vars.doc: the number of employees fired in each season

description: The total discounted cost gathers the season-by-season costs into a single figure. For every season, take that season's direct production cost together with its inventory cost and its workforce cost, bring that seasonal total into present value using the season's discount factor, and set the overall total cost equal to the sum of these discounted seasonal totals across all seasons.

description: The direct production cost of a season comes from materials and from running the shifts. For each season, charge the material cost for every motor produced that season, add for each shift the fixed cost of operating that shift when it runs together with the labor cost for each employee on that shift, and express the result in thousands of dollars. Set the season's direct production cost equal to this amount.

description: Production in a season is read off the piecewise-linear production curve. For each season, the motors produced equal the weighted combination of the motors-produced values at the curve levels, where the weights are those placed on each level across all shifts. Set the season's production equal to this weighted total.

description: The employees on a shift are read off the same production curve. For each season and each shift, the headcount on that shift equals the weighted combination of the employee-hours values at the curve levels, using the weights placed on each level for that shift. description: The interpolation weights for a shift are tied to whether that shift is operated. For each season and each shift, the weights placed across the curve levels must add up to whether the shift runs, so the weights sum to one when the shift is operated and to zero when it is not.

description: Inventory carries motors forward from one season to the next. For each season, the inventory held at the end of the season equals the inventory carried in from the previous season plus what was produced that season minus what was demanded that season. In the opening season there is no previous season, so nothing is carried in.
expected_pyomo:
def invb_rule(model, q):
    qlist = list(model.q)
    idx = qlist.index(q)
    prev = model.inv[qlist[idx-1]] if idx > 0 else 0
    return model.inv[q] == prev + model.p[q] - model.d[q]
model.invb = Constraint(model.q, rule=invb_rule)

Production in each season is set by interpolating along a piecewise-linear production curve. Costs accrue each season for materials and shift operation, for carrying inventory and leasing space, and for hiring and firing, and every season's cost is expressed in present value using a discount factor. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon.

description: The inventory cost of a season combines the lease charge with the space-rental charge. For each season, add the season's leasing cost whenever the lease is taken to that season's space-rental charge, express the result in thousands of dollars, and set the season's inventory cost equal to it.

model_narrative: We plan motor production across the seasons of a year. In each season we decide how many motors to produce, which shifts to operate and how many employees to put on each shift, how many employees to hire or fire, how much inventory to carry forward, and whether to take an extra-space lease for the year. Production in each season is set by interpolating along a piecewise-linear production curve. Costs accrue each season for materials and shift operation, for carrying inventory and leasing space, and for hiring and firing, and every season's cost is expressed in present value using a discount factor. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon.

description: The workforce evolves from season to season through hiring and firing. For each season, the headcount equals the headcount carried in from the previous season plus the employees hired that season minus the employees fired that season plus any externally given change to the headcount entering that season. In the opening season there is no previous season, so no headcount is carried in.
expected_pyomo:
def eb1_rule(model, q):
    qlist = list(model.q)
    idx = qlist.index(q)
    prev_e = model.e[qlist[idx-1]] if idx > 0 else 0
    return model.e[q] == prev_e + model.h[q] - model.f[q] + model.ei[q]
model.eb1 = Constraint(model.q, rule=eb1_rule)

The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon.

description: The interpolation weights may only be placed on adjacent levels of the production curve. For each season, shift, and level, the weight on this level together with the weight on the level just below it cannot exceed the combined weight on this level, the level just below it, and the level two below it. At the lowest levels, where there is no level below or only one, the comparison applies over whatever lower levels exist.

params.doc: the breakpoint coordinates of the piecewise-linear production curve at each level: the 'labor' row gives the employee-hours value and the 'motor' row gives the motors-produced value at that level; indexed first by the row label then by level

params.name: sc
params.doc: shift cost coefficients: the 'fixed' row gives the fixed cost of operating a shift and the 'labor' row gives the per-employee labor cost of a shift; indexed first by the row label then by shift

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total discounted cost over the whole horizon, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: dpc
vars.doc: the direct production cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: isc
vars.doc: the inventory-related cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: wfc
vars.doc: the workforce-adjustment cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: src
vars.doc: the space-rental charge in each season, in dollars

vars.name: p
vars.doc: the number of motors produced in each season

vars.name: ss
vars.doc: the interpolation weight placed on each production-curve level for a given season and shift; the weights select a point along the piecewise-linear curve

vars.name: ssb
vars.doc: an auxiliary binary indicator for the piecewise-linear segment of each season, shift, and level; not referenced by any constraint in this model

vars.name: inv
vars.doc: the inventory of motors carried at the end of each season

vars.name: lease
vars.doc: the lease decision; equals 1 if the extra-space lease is taken and 0 otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: the total workforce headcount employed in each season

vars.name: se
vars.doc: the workforce headcount assigned to each shift in each season

vars.name: shift
vars.doc: the shift-operation indicator; equals 1 if the shift is operated in that season and 0 otherwise

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the number of employees hired in each season

vars.name: f
vars.doc: the number of employees fired in each season

description: Generate the complete constraint set for this model.
